title: Research
text:
My primary research agenda examines how student loan debt shapes political attitudes and behaviors. This work investigates:

How debt burdens influence political participation
The relationship between student debt and political attitudes
Policy preferences among student loan borrowers

title: Alexander Ross
text:
Email
  
  
    
     CV
  
  
    
     Google Scholar
  

  
  
I am a postdoctoral researcher at Université Laval. I received my PhD in Political Science from UC Riverside in 2024.
My research focuses on the politics of personal debt, specifically student loan debt, and how debt impacts the political attitudes and behaviors of individuals.
Prior to my doctoral studies, I earned a Master’s in Public Policy. I have also taught English in Beijing and served with AmeriCorps.

title: Research
text:
Current Project

PASI (Programme d’accompagnement et de soutien à l’intégration)
I am currently working with Prof. Valérie-Anne Mahéo at Université Laval on a project analyzing a novel panel dataset on recent immigrants to Quebec. This project, funded by MIFI (Quebec’s immigration government agency), surveys participants recruited through administrative data to study the impact of initial experiences on later political and social integration and political attitudes.
This is the first panel dataset on political attitudes composed exclusively of recently arrived immigrants in Canada or the US.
